title: Timeline and Date Columns - Please give me feedback
excerpt:

posts.author: TJ_S (TJS)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday community,

Need help on this.

We are using Date column for our due date/deadline for every line item. And we do dependency for every line item.
However, we can’t find a way to skip weekends/holidays. Is there a separate automation to do this? Please help.

Thank you.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @TJ_S - I had a look at this in my test environment and it would always skip over the weekends.

Item 1: Due Date = Thurs. June 3
Item 2: Due Date = Friday June 4 / Dependancy on Item 1

I then changed Item 1 Due Date to Friday June 4, the Due Date of Item 2 was set to Monday June 7.

Have you checked your settings under Admin > General > Account Tab. Make sure Hide Weekends is set. As for holidays, that would be trickier as holidays are region specific.

image794×452 37 KB

posts.author: TJ_S (TJS)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi

Thank you for this. However, we don’t use Timeline Column for our Due Date options. We use the Date column. Because timeline column has very less filtering options:

  • Timeline Filters:
  • Due Today
  • Future
  • Past
  • Milestone
  • Blank

As compare to Date Column:

  • Overdue
  • Done on time
  • Done overdue
  • Today
  • Tomorrow
  • Yesterday
  • This week
  • Last Week
  • Next Week
  • Last Month
  • Next Month
  • Past Dates
  • Future Dates
  • Upcoming
  • Blank

Meanwhile using both columns in the board is not as efficient as it can be because date plotting is very manual.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TJ_S - sorry, in my example I was using Date columns not timeline columns, so it should work I think. I recorded a quick video to show it working as expected in action:

Let me know how it goes,
Mark

posts.author: TJ_S (TJS)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Mark! I really appreciate you taking your time on this. Now, I realized that this would work for Date column only if setting Monday as the first day of the week. So, I think, we have to switch to that settings. We have been using Sunday as the first day of the week because it is visually common for everyone.

Thanks again. Much appreciated.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: My pleasure @TJ_S glad you got it sorted!
